The **B postcode area**, also known as the **Birmingham postcode area**,[[2]](#cite_note-2) is a group of 79 postcode districts in central England, within 15 post towns. These cover the central portion of the [West Midlands](/wiki/West_Midlands_(county) "West Midlands (county)") (including [Birmingham](/wiki/Birmingham "Birmingham"), [West Bromwich](/wiki/West_Bromwich "West Bromwich"), [Sutton Coldfield](/wiki/Sutton_Coldfield "Sutton Coldfield"), [Smethwick](/wiki/Smethwick "Smethwick"), [Solihull](/wiki/Solihull "Solihull"), [Halesowen](/wiki/Halesowen "Halesowen"), [Cradley Heath](/wiki/Cradley_Heath "Cradley Heath"), [Oldbury](/wiki/Oldbury,_West_Midlands "Oldbury, West Midlands") and [Rowley Regis](/wiki/Rowley_Regis "Rowley Regis")), plus north-east [Worcestershire](/wiki/Worcestershire "Worcestershire") (including [Bromsgrove](/wiki/Bromsgrove "Bromsgrove") and [Redditch](/wiki/Redditch "Redditch")), north and west [Warwickshire](/wiki/Warwickshire "Warwickshire") (including [Alcester](/wiki/Alcester "Alcester"), [Studley](/wiki/Studley,_Warwickshire "Studley, Warwickshire") and [Henley-in-Arden](/wiki/Henley-in-Arden "Henley-in-Arden")), and part of south-east [Staffordshire](/wiki/Staffordshire "Staffordshire") (including [Tamworth](/wiki/Tamworth,_Staffordshire "Tamworth, Staffordshire")) and a very small part of [Leicestershire](/wiki/Leicestershire "Leicestershire"). It is the [most populated postcode area in the United Kingdom](/wiki/List_of_postcode_areas_in_the_United_Kingdom_by_population "List of postcode areas in the United Kingdom by population"), with a population of over 1.9 million.

## Former scheme

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=B_postcode_area&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Former scheme")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/83/BirdbrookRoadSigns.jpg/250px-BirdbrookRoadSigns.jpg)](/wiki/File:BirdbrookRoadSigns.jpg)

Street name signs on Birdbrook Road, Great Barr, Birmingham, showing old "Birmingham 22" (top) and modern "B44" postcodes.

Before the introduction of postcodes in the 1960s, Birmingham along with other major cities were divided into numbered postal districts. With a few exceptions these were directly incorporated into the outcode (the first part of the postcode).

For example, [Great Barr](/wiki/Great_Barr "Great Barr") was formerly *Birmingham 22* (now B43 and 44) and [Smethwick](/wiki/Smethwick "Smethwick") was formerly Smethwick 40 and 41 (now B66 and 67).
